Class PaginationRequest

java.lang.Object
org.apache.axis2.json.rpc.PaginationRequest
public class PaginationRequest extends Object
Pagination parameters that a client sends with a list query.

Services can embed these fields directly in their request POJO or accept a PaginationRequest as a separate parameter. The values map directly to Hibernate/JPA's setFirstResult(offset) and setMaxResults(limit).

Defaults

  • offset = 0 — start from the beginning
  • limit = 50 — return at most 50 items

Safety

The getLimit() getter clamps the value to
invalid reference 
#MAX_LIMIT
(default 2000) to prevent clients from requesting unbounded result sets that could exhaust server memory. Services can override this by calling setMaxLimit(int) before reading the limit.

Wire format

When embedded in a request POJO: 
{
  "searchTerm": "AAPL",
  "offset": 100,
  "limit": 50
}

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    DEFAULT_LIMIT
    Default page size when not specified by the client.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_MAX_LIMIT
    Maximum page size to prevent unbounded queries.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_LIMIT

      public static final int DEFAULT_LIMIT
      Default page size when not specified by the client.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • DEFAULT_MAX_LIMIT

      public static final int DEFAULT_MAX_LIMIT
      Maximum page size to prevent unbounded queries. Set to 2000 to match common enterprise grid configurations where large virtual-scroll pages are used to minimize round trips while staying within reasonable memory.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • PaginationRequest

      public PaginationRequest()

    • PaginationRequest

      public PaginationRequest(int offset, int limit)

  • Method Details

    • getOffset

      public int getOffset()
      Zero-based offset. Negative values are clamped to 0.

    • setOffset

      public void setOffset(int offset)

    • getLimit

      public int getLimit()
      Page size. Clamped to [1, maxLimit]. If the client sends 0 or negative, the default (50) is used. If the client sends more than maxLimit, it is capped.

    • setLimit

      public void setLimit(int limit)

    • setMaxLimit

      public void setMaxLimit(int maxLimit)
      Override the maximum allowed limit for this request. Call this before getLimit() to change the cap.

    • getMaxLimit

      public int getMaxLimit()
